sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: BofA is Bullish on CRISPR Therapeutics AG (CRSP)
feed.summary: BofA maintained a buy rating on CRISPR Therapeutics AG (CRSP) despite slightly lowering its price objective from $90 to $89 due to revised Casgevy 2026 projections. The firm still anticipates consecutive improvement for Casgevy throughout 2026 and expects regulatory submissions for younger patients and advancements in other clinical programs. CRISPR Therapeutics announced strategic priorities for 2026, including Casgevy's global commercialization and clinical trial initiations for new therapies.

feed.title: Dover beats Q4 expectations, shares rise on strong organic growth
feed.summary: Dover Corporation exceeded analyst expectations for its fourth-quarter earnings, driven by strong organic revenue growth across its diverse portfolio. The company reported adjusted EPS of $2.51 and revenue of $2.1 billion, leading to a 1.46% rise in pre-market trading. Dover also provided a positive outlook for 2026, forecasting continued revenue and earnings growth, supported by productivity initiatives and capital allocation strategies.

feed.title: Tesla Finally Announced the End of an Era
feed.summary: During its Q4 earnings report, Tesla announced it would stop production of the Model S and Model X cars to convert its Fremont factory to produce Optimus robots. While the company reported a decline in revenue and profits, its stock rose by two percent. This move marks the end of an era for the Model S, which was instrumental in establishing the viability of the electric vehicle market.

feed.title: L3Harris: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies, Inc. reported fourth-quarter net income of $300 million, or $1.59 per share, with adjusted earnings of $2.86 per share, surpassing analyst estimates. Despite exceeding earnings expectations, the company's revenue of $5.65 billion for the quarter fell short of Wall Street forecasts. For the full year, L3Harris posted a profit of $1.61 billion on revenues of $21.87 billion, and provided full-year earnings guidance between $11.30 and $11.50 per share with revenues projected between $23 billion and $23.5 billion.

feed.title: Altria aims for 'progressive' dividend rate growth (MO:NYSE)
feed.summary: Altria Group (MO) experienced a dip in early trading after its fourth-quarter earnings fell slightly short of profit estimates, with net revenue decreasing by 2.1% largely due to lower smokeable product sales. Despite this, the company aims for a "progressive" dividend rate growth, forecasting 2.5% to 5.5% EPS growth for 2026 and a mid-single-digit EPS CAGR through 2028. Risks include declining cigarette shipment volumes, competition from illicit e-vapor products, and pressure on consumer spending.

feed.title: A.O. Smith tops Q4 earnings estimates, issues 2026 guidance By Investing.com
feed.summary: A.O. Smith Corporation (NYSE:AOS) exceeded analyst expectations for its fourth-quarter earnings, reporting an EPS of $0.90 against an estimated $0.84. While revenue of $912.5 million slightly missed forecasts, the company achieved a 20% increase in diluted EPS year-over-year due to pricing benefits and improved profitability in both North America and the Rest of World segments. A.O. Smith also issued 2026 guidance, projecting revenue between $3.9 billion and $4.02 billion and EPS from $3.85 to $4.15, with the midpoint representing a 4% increase over 2025.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin and U.S. Department of War Sign Framework Agreement to Quadruple THAAD Interceptor Production Capacity
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin and the U.S. Department of War have signed a framework agreement to quadruple the annual production of THAAD interceptors from 96 to 400. This builds on a previous agreement for PAC-3 MSE interceptors and coincides with Lockheed Martin breaking ground on a new Munitions Acceleration Center in Arkansas. The company plans a multibillion-dollar investment over the next three years to expand capacity and modernize facilities, creating thousands of American jobs to meet increasing defense demands.

feed.title: Ameriprise Financial declares quarterly dividend of $1.60 per share
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial (NYSE: AMP) has announced a quarterly cash dividend of $1.60 per common share, payable on February 27, 2026, to shareholders of record by February 9, 2026. The company boasts a 21-year consecutive dividend raise history and currently has a "GOOD" financial health rating according to InvestingPro. Recent strategic initiatives include expanding its board and welcoming new advisor teams.

feed.title: Valero Energy beats quarterly estimates for profit on stronger refining margins By Reuters
feed.summary: Valero Energy surpassed Wall Street's profit expectations for the fourth quarter, driven by stronger refining margins and increased throughput volume. The company's shares rose 1.6% pre-bell after reporting a refining margin of $13.61 per barrel, up from $8.44 a year ago. Valero also announced progress on a $230 million FCC Unit optimization project at its St. Charles Refinery, expected to enhance high-value product production.

feed.title: L3Harris Technologies earnings beat by $0.09, revenue fell short of estimates

The company provided FY 2026 EPS guidance of $11.30-$11.50, below the analyst consensus of $12.39. Despite the revenue miss and lower guidance, the stock has shown strong performance over the last 3 and 12 months.

feed.title: Investing in MetLife (NYSE:MET) Five Years Ago Would Have Delivered You a 80% Gain

The company's revenue growth has also been modest at 1.2% annually, leading analysts to suggest further investigation into the business's progress.
